description: Setting contact models and contact properties in PFC 5.0 using cmat, contact model, and contact property commands. Covers default models, deformability method, parallel bonds, and type-specific defaults.

sections.heading: CRITICAL: PFC5 Default Contact Model is Null
sections.content:
In PFC 5.0, the default contact model is the Null model — no forces are transmitted between contacting pieces until you specify a contact model. This is a critical difference from PFC 4.0 (which defaulted to linear).

ALWAYS specify cmat before cycling:
  cmat default model linear
  cmat default model linear method deformability emod 1e9 kratio 1.0
  cmat default property fric 0.5 dp_nratio 0.2

Available contact models in PFC 5.0 (12 total):
  - null (default — no forces)
  - linear (elastic-frictional)
  - linearcbond (contact bond, force only)
  - linearpbond (parallel bond, force + moment)
  - hertz (nonlinear elastic)
  - hysteretic (loading/unloading)
  - smoothjoint (reduced interlocking)
  - flatjoint (finite-size interface)
  - rrlinear (rolling resistance)
  - burger (viscoelastic)
  - thermalnull (thermal null)
  - thermalpipe (thermal conduction)

For detailed PFC5-specific properties and syntax of each model,
see 'PFC5 Contact Models Complete Reference' (pfc5-contact-models.json)
in the pfc5-guide.

sections.heading: cmat default — Setting Default Contact Models
sections.content:
The 'cmat default' command (PFC 5.0 syntax; 'contact cmat default' in PFC 7.0+) sets the contact model that will be assigned to new contacts as they form.

Syntax:
  cmat default model <model_name> [property <prop> <value> ...]

Common models:
  linear            — linear elastic (no tension)
  linearpbond       — linear elastic + parallel bond (bonds carry force/moment)
  linearcbond       — linear elastic + contact bond (point bond, no moment)
  hertz             — Hertz-Mindlin (nonlinear elastic)
  smoothjoint       — smooth joint for jointed rock
  flatjoint         — flat joint for rock
  rollingresistance — rolling resistance for sand

Examples:
  cmat default model linear property kn 1.0e6 ks 1.0e6 fric 0.5
  cmat default model linearpbond property pb_ten 10.0e6 pb_coh 50.0e6 pb_fa 0.0

sections.heading: Deformability Method
sections.content:
The deformability method automatically computes kn and ks from Young's modulus (E) and stiffness ratio (kn/ks).

Syntax:
  cmat default model linear method deformability emod E kratio R

  emod E    — Young's modulus (stress units)
  kratio R  — kn/ks ratio (kratio = 0 means ks = kn, i.e., no shear stiffness distinction)

For ball-ball contacts:
  kn = 2 * E * r_avg   (where r_avg = (r1 + r2) / 2 for two contacting balls)
  ks = kn / kratio

For ball-facet contacts (ball-to-wall):
  kn = 4 * E * r_ball   (wall is treated as infinite radius)
  ks = kn / kratio

Example:
  cmat default model linear method deformability emod 1.0e9 kratio 2.5

Note: The deformability method must be called BEFORE cmat apply or ball generation to take effect. It does not modify existing contacts.

sections.heading: Type-Specific CMAT Defaults
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 cmat supports setting different default models by contact type:

  cmat default type ball-ball model linearpbond property fric 0.577 kn 1e8 ks 1e8 pb_kn 1e8 pb_ks 1e8 pb_ten 1e6 pb_coh 1e6 pb_rmul 0.8 dp_nratio 0.2
  cmat default type ball-facet model linear property fric 0.1 kn 1e8 ks 1e8 dp_nratio 0.2

Contact types:
  ball-ball       — between two balls
  ball-facet       — between ball and wall facet
  ball-pebble      — between ball and clump pebble (if clumps present)
  pebble-facet     — between clump pebble and wall
  pebble-pebble    — between pebbles of different clumps

When both type-specific and general defaults exist, the type-specific default takes precedence for that contact type.

sections.heading: Installing Bonds with contact method bond
sections.content:
After assigning a bonded contact model (linearpbond), use contact method to install bonds:

  contact model linearpbond range contact type ball-ball
  contact method bond gap 0.1
  contact method deformability emod 1e9 kratio 1.0
  contact method pb_deform emod 1e9 kratio 1.0
  contact property pb_ten 10e6 pb_coh 50e6 pb_fa 0.0
  contact property fric 0.577

The 'gap' parameter in 'contact method bond' controls the maximum separation distance for bond installation. Bonds are created only between pieces that are closer than the gap distance (including overlaps where gap < 0).

PFC5 uses separate contact method commands for:
  - contact method deformability emod E krat R  — set ball-ball stiffness via Young's modulus
  - contact method pb_deform emod E krat R     — set parallel-bond stiffness via Young's modulus
  - contact method bond gap G                  — install bonds with gap tolerance

Note: In PFC5 these are TOP-LEVEL contact commands ('contact method ...'), not sub-commands of 'contact property'.

sections.heading: cmat apply — Applying Defaults to Existing Contacts
sections.content:
After setting defaults, use 'cmat apply' to assign the default models to all existing contacts that don't already have a model assigned.

Syntax:
  cmat apply [range ...]

Without range, applies to all contacts. With range, applies only to contacts matching the range condition.

Example:
  cmat default model linearpbond property pb_ten 10.0e6
  cmat apply

Note: cmat apply only assigns the model to contacts that have no model. To change an existing contact model, use 'contact model'.

sections.heading: contact model — Changing Existing Contacts
sections.content:
Change the contact model of existing contacts that already have a model.

Syntax:
  contact model <model_name> range contact type <ball-ball|ball-facet> [...]

  range contact type  — filter by contact type
  range ...           — additional range filters

Example:
  contact model linearpbond range contact type ball-ball
  contact model linearpbond range contact type ball-facet group 'sample' slot 'material'

sections.heading: contact method — Setting Stiffness Method for Existing Contacts
sections.content:
Apply deformability or bonding method to existing contacts.

  contact method bond gap G
    — Activates bonding for contacts with gap < G. Only affects contact-bonded models.
    — For linearpbond: when gap < G and bond is activated, the bond carries the parallel force.
    — gap G: activation distance (negative = overlap required; positive = small gap allowed).

  contact method deform emod E krat R
    — Sets kn/ks via deformability for existing contacts (ball-ball and ball-facet).
    — emod E: Young's modulus
    — krat R: kn/ks ratio

  contact method pb_deform emod E krat R
    — Sets parallel bond stiffness via deformability for existing linearpbond contacts.
    — emod E: parallel bond modulus
    — krat R: pb_kn/pb_ks ratio

Examples:
  contact method bond gap -0.001 range contact type ball-ball
  contact method deform emod 1.0e9 krat 2.5
  contact method pb_deform emod 1.0e9 krat 2.5

sections.heading: contact property — Setting Contact Properties
sections.content:
Set or modify contact properties directly.

Syntax:
  contact property <prop_name> <value> [<prop_name> <value> ...] [range ...]

Common linear model properties:
  kn K      — normal stiffness (force/length)
  ks K      — shear stiffness (force/length)
  fric F    — friction coefficient (0.0 = frictionless)

Common linearpbond additional properties:
  pb_ten T  — parallel bond tensile strength (stress)
  pb_coh C  — parallel bond cohesion (stress)
  pb_fa A   — parallel bond friction angle (degrees)
  pb_kn K   — parallel bond normal stiffness (stress/length)
  pb_ks K   — parallel bond shear stiffness (stress/length)

Examples:
  contact property kn 1.0e7 ks 1.0e7 fric 0.7
  contact property pb_ten 5.0e6 pb_coh 25.0e6 range contact type ball-ball

sections.heading: Viewing Contact Information
sections.content:
Use the following commands to inspect contacts:
  contact list              — list all contacts with basic info
  contact list model        — show contact models
  contact list property     — show contact properties
  contact list range ...    — filter by range

In FISH, access contact information via:
  contact.find(id)          — find contact by ID
  contact.pos(cp, dir)      — contact position
  contact.force.n(cp)       — normal contact force
  contact.force.s(cp)       — shear contact force magnitude
  contact.gap(cp)           — contact gap (negative = overlap)
  contact.surf1(cp)         — first surface pointer
  contact.surf2(cp)         — second surface pointer

sections.heading: Practical Notes
sections.content:
- cmat commands must be issued BEFORE contacts form (before ball generation) to take effect on new contacts.
- After ball generation, use 'cmat apply' to propagate defaults.
- To change an existing contact's model, use 'contact model ...' followed by 'contact method ...' and 'contact property ...'.
- The deformability method is preferred over manual kn/ks specification because it automatically scales with particle size.
- For cohesive materials (rock, cemented soil), use 'linearpbond' model. For granular materials, use 'linear' or 'rollingresistance'.
- In PFC 5.0, always set 'cmat default' before 'ball distribute' or 'ball generate' to ensure proper contact model assignment.
- The Null model is the PFC5 default. If you cycle without setting cmat, no forces will be calculated and particles will simply pass through each other.
